principles: Coverage is catalog-wide and generated from the authoritative command catalog.
Level 0 is fully authored for beginners.
Levels 1-39 are honest planned outlines until real evidence is authored.
Aliases never create separate learning identities.

mastery_policy.reason: Orientation does not award command mastery.

review_eligibility.reason: Concept review only.

modules.title: Beginner Orientation
learning_objectives: Build confidence before vendor syntax.
Separate production devices from local practice.

lessons.title: Mission: What a network technician protects
lessons.objective: Describe the technician job as protecting communication with evidence, patience, and safe choices.
lessons.mission: A guest cannot connect. Your job is to collect clues, explain what they mean, and choose a safe next step.
lessons.learn: Beginner learning starts with concepts and evidence before vendor syntax or configuration changes.
lessons.see: Endpoint -> access switch -> gateway -> service. The path gives every check a place.
lessons.predict: Say what a healthy result should look like before reading output.
lessons.try_prompt: Choose the safest answer from the local training prompt.
lessons.explain: A useful answer names the symptom, evidence, interpretation, and next step.

lessons.title: Learn: The words beginners hear first
lessons.objective: Recognize common words used by network teams without memorizing vendor commands yet.
lessons.mission: Follow the beginner path by separating words, evidence, prediction, safe practice, and clear notes.

lessons.try_prompt: Choose the safest answer from the local training prompt.
